penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Fourth video (V20, V22, V26, V29) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. GPT-4o's rubric-strict 2.5 is the correct rubric application. Recommend formalizing the rubric-allowed penalty set in the prompt and adding a separate cosmetic_quality field if Claude's signal is worth capturing.

phase_attribution_disagreement.claude_diagnosis: Phase split: load+placement 25s, throws 175s (55+60+60), cut 95s. Cutting and throws 2/3 are the loss centers.
phase_attribution_disagreement.gpt4o_diagnosis: Phase split: needle_load 24s, placement 36s, first_throw 20s, second_throw 65s, third_throw 61s, cut 97s. Same loss centers but first throw is moved out of throws and into the placement window.
phase_attribution_disagreement.analysis: Same boundary-disagreement pattern as V21/V24: tails converge but Claude bundles ~35s of first-throw setup into the upstream phase while GPT-4o keeps it in first_throw. Cutting durations agree within 2s and throws 2/3 agree within 5s each. Diagnosis is functionally identical.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Strong score agreement (delta 4.0). Reconciled value is 293.0 (GPT-4o, rubric-strict). The 4-point delta is entirely Claude's out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. Both teachers independently treat V29 as a fatigue/equipment-adaptation regression on the Vizio station rather than a skill regression.

systematic_findings.v28_v29_adaptation_arc_confirmed: Both teachers independently identify V29 as a 70-second improvement over V28 (365 to 295/304.5) and frame it as equipment adaptation rather than skill regression. This is the strongest single-attempt improvement signal in the series.
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_recurring: Fourth case (V20, V22, V26, V29) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. Pattern is now persistent enough to recommend prompt-level formalization of the allowed penalty set.
systematic_findings.largest_confidence_gap_in_series: GPT-4o 0.64 vs Claude 0.30 = delta 0.34, the largest confidence gap recorded. Claude penalizes the Vizio moire/color-banding artifacts much harder than GPT-4o does. Claude's lower number is the more defensible read on this recording.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_continues: V29 is another drain-closure agreement case. Running tally: AGREE 8 (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V26, V27, V29), DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).
systematic_findings.phase_split_drift_recurs: Boundary disagreement on first_throw vs preceding phase recurs (also seen on V21, V24). Same convergent-tails pattern: totals agree, internal allocation differs by ~30-40s on one specific boundary.
